_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to conduct and implement market research to understand government and industry capabilities and appropriate pricing.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to select the appropriate analytical method for a given business question, balancing statistical rigor, time constraints, data availability, and audience comprehension capacity.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI AB domain. AB-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-A-005 (LS, cosine: 0.507), DG-A-005 (DG, cosine: 0.473).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.8/10 (raw 0.379). Shared vocabulary: [ability, appropriate]. DCWF-only terms [capabilities, conduct, government, implement, industry, market] vs WIDAI-only terms [analytical, audience, availability, balancing, business, capacity] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.875 (P=0.884, R=0.866). Balanced P/R — the 16-word DCWF element and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.4%). "Ability to conduct and implement market research to understand government and in" and "Ability to select the appropriate analytical method for a given business questio" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.513 > STS 0.379 (gap +0.134). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ability, appropriate] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 12.5%. Shared tokens: [ability, appropriate]. DCWF-only: [capabilities, conduct, government, implement, industry, market]. WIDAI-only: [analytical, audience, availability, balancing, business, capacity].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.134 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
